title: Nexus Mutual Cover Router API Vocabulary
description: Domain terminology and concept definitions for the Nexus Mutual decentralized insurance protocol and Cover Router API.

terms.definition: A decentralized insurance policy purchased from the Nexus Mutual protocol that protects a member against specified risk events such as smart contract hacks, custody failure, slashing, or depeg.
A specific insurable risk category offered by the Nexus Mutual protocol (e.g., smart contract cover for a specific protocol). Each product has a unique productId.
A smart contract pool of NXM tokens staked by Nexus Mutual members who underwrite risk. Each pool can provide capacity for one or more cover products at a given price.
The maximum amount of cover that can be purchased for a given product, limited by the total NXM staked in pools underwriting that product.
A calculated premium estimate for a specific cover request, including the total premium in NXM and the cover asset, and the optimal pool allocation to fulfil the cover.
The price paid by a cover buyer for a Nexus Mutual policy. Can be paid in NXM or the cover asset (ETH, DAI, USDC, etc.).
An integer identifier uniquely identifying a cover product within the Nexus Mutual protocol.
An integer identifier uniquely identifying a staking pool within the Nexus Mutual protocol.
The asset in which a cover amount is denominated. Identified by an integer assetId (e.g., 0 = ETH, 1 = DAI). Determines the unit for premiumInAsset.
The duration of a cover policy expressed in days. Must be between 28 and 365 days (inclusive). Defaults to 30 days when not specified.
The amount of NXM tokens already committed to active covers within a pool or product. Expressed as a string integer in NXM wei.
The annual premium rate for a cover, expressed as an integer in basis points (0-10,000). Divide by 10,000 to obtain the decimal rate (e.g., 250 = 2.5% per year).
The minimum annual price available across all pools for a product, expressed as a decimal fraction between 0 and 1 (e.g., 0.025 = 2.5%).
The maximum annual price available across any pool for a product, expressed as a decimal fraction between 0 and 1 (e.g., 0.1 = 10%).
The intended annual price set by a staking pool manager for a specific product, expressed in basis points (0-10,000).
The proportion of a staking pool's capacity currently committed to active covers, expressed in basis points (0-10,000). A value of 5000 indicates 50% utilization.
An array of pool-level cover allocation instructions returned in a quote and required as input data for the on-chain buy-cover transaction.
The native governance and staking token of the Nexus Mutual protocol, used by members to stake in pools, vote on claims, and optionally pay premiums.
An optional integer identifying an existing cover being modified (renewed or increased). When provided, existing allocation in pools is excluded from capacity checks.
The capacity-weighted mean of target prices across all pools that can underwrite a given product, expressed in basis points (0-10,000).

The Nexus Mutual off-chain service that optimally allocates a cover request across staking pools to minimise the buyer's premium. The Cover Router REST API exposes its capacity, pricing, and quote endpoints.
